zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex於1061年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾時，她的父親Geoffrey de Mandeville26歲，母親Adeliza Balts22歲。她至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於大約1097年4月19日在英國英格蘭艾可利可林享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex于1061年出生在英国英格兰奧可福爾时，她的父亲Geoffrey de Mandeville26岁，母亲Adeliza Balts22岁。她至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于大约1097年4月19日在英国英格兰艾可利可林享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex was born in 1061, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Geoffrey de Mandeville, was 26 and her mother, Adeliza Balts, was 22. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 19 April 1097, in Rickling, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex est née en 1061, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffrey de Mandeville, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Adeliza Balts, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 19 avril 1097, à Rickling, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex 1061 in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Geoffrey de Mandeville 26 und ihre Mutter Adeliza Balts 22. Sie hatte mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 19. April 1097 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Rickling, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex è nata il nel 1061 nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Geoffrey de Mandeville, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Adeliza Balts, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 19 aprile 1097, nel Rickling, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essexが1061年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de Mandevilleは26歳，母Adeliza Baltsは22歳であった。彼女は少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1097年4月19日にイギリスイギリスエセックスリックリングで36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex(이)가 1061년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Geoffrey de Mandeville(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adeliza Balts(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1097년 4월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 릭링에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex nasceu em 1061, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Geoffrey de Mandeville, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Adeliza Balts, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 19 de abril de 1097, em Rickling, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Beatrice de Mandeville Countess of Essex родилась в 1061 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Geoffrey de Mandeville было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Adeliza Balts, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 19 апреля 1097 г. в местности рикклинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 36 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 19 de abril de 1097, en Rickling, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 36 años.
